concept: SessionReady lifecycle transition sets lifecycle to Ready
notes: Mutation: change SessionLifecycle.Ready to SessionLifecycle.CreationFailed — no scenario catches this lifecycle field assignment. Operator: replace-value.
assertion: After SessionReady, state.lifecycle === 'ready'

concept: SessionCreationFailed sets lifecycle to CreationFailed and attaches error
notes: Mutation: negate lifecycle assignment; or remove creationError assignment. Two orthogonal mutation targets in one action.
assertion: After SessionCreationFailed, state.lifecycle === 'creationFailed' AND state.creationError equals action.error

concept: SessionTurnStarted creates activeTurn with correct turnId and message
notes: Mutation: omit responseParts initialisation to non-empty array, or assign wrong id. Operator: remove-initializer, replace-value.
assertion: After SessionTurnStarted, activeTurn.id === action.turnId AND activeTurn.responseParts.length === 0

concept: SessionTurnStarted with queuedMessageId removes matching steeringMessage
notes: Mutation: remove the steeringMessage clearing branch. A surviving mutant here means the steering message lingers after the turn it triggered is started.
assertion: After SessionTurnStarted with queuedMessageId, state.steeringMessage should be undefined when it matched

concept: SessionTurnStarted removes the triggering message from queuedMessages list

citation.excerpt: if (next.queuedMessages) { const filtered = next.queuedMessages.filter(m => m.id !== action.queuedMessageId);

notes: Mutation: remove filter — queuedMessages retains all entries. Or negate the id comparison.
assertion: After SessionTurnStarted with queuedMessageId, the matched queued entry is absent from queuedMessages

concept: SessionDelta appends content to existing Markdown response part
notes: Mutation: replace concatenation with assignment (part.content = action.content) — content is replaced not appended. Conditional-boundary / replace-operator.
assertion: After two SessionDelta actions, part.content equals first.content + second.content

concept: SessionDelta on non-Markdown part returns part unchanged

citation.excerpt: if (part.kind === ResponsePartKind.Markdown) { return { ...part, content: part.content + action.content }; } return part;

notes: Mutation: remove the kind check — delta would modify any part. Operator: remove-conditional.
assertion: SessionDelta on a ToolCall part does not change the toolCall field

concept: SessionResponsePart is no-op when active turn id does not match
notes: Mutation: remove the early-return guard — a stale turnId would still append to the active turn. Operator: remove-conditional.
assertion: SessionResponsePart with wrong turnId leaves state unchanged

concept: SessionTurnComplete moves activeTurn to turns array with state Complete
notes: Mutation: pass TurnState.Cancelled instead — turn.state would be wrong. Operator: replace-value.
assertion: After SessionTurnComplete, turns[last].state === 'complete' and activeTurn === undefined

concept: SessionTurnCancelled moves activeTurn to turns array with state Cancelled
notes: Mutation: pass TurnState.Complete instead. Operator: replace-value.
assertion: After SessionTurnCancelled, turns[last].state === 'cancelled'

concept: SessionError moves activeTurn to turns with state Error and attaches error

citation.excerpt: return endTurn(state, action.turnId, TurnState.Error, SessionStatus.Error, action.error);

notes: Mutation: remove action.error arg — error field not set on turn. Operator: remove-argument.
assertion: After SessionError, turns[last].state === 'error' AND turns[last].error matches action.error

concept: endTurn helper returns state unchanged when activeTurn.id does not match turnId
notes: Mutation: remove the id-mismatch guard — endTurn would always finalize whatever turn is active. Affects SessionTurnComplete, SessionTurnCancelled, SessionError paths.
assertion: SessionTurnComplete with wrong turnId leaves activeTurn unchanged and does not append to turns

concept: endTurn forces non-terminal tool calls to Cancelled on turn completion

citation.excerpt: if (tc.status === ToolCallStatus.Completed || tc.status === ToolCallStatus.Cancelled) { return part; }

notes: Mutation: remove the completed/cancelled guard — already-terminal tool calls get overwritten. Or replace Cancelled with Completed in the forced transition.
assertion: After SessionTurnComplete, a Streaming tool call in the active turn becomes Cancelled, not Completed or Streaming

concept: SessionToolCallStart adds a new ToolCall part with status Streaming
notes: Mutation: set status to Running. Operator: replace-enum-value.
assertion: After SessionToolCallStart, the new tool call part has status Streaming

concept: SessionToolCallDelta appends to partialInput only when status is Streaming
notes: Mutation: remove Streaming guard — non-Streaming tool calls also accumulate partialInput. Or replace concatenation with assignment.
assertion: SessionToolCallDelta appends content; a second delta on same tool call yields both contents concatenated

concept: SessionToolCallReady without confirmed flag transitions to PendingConfirmation
notes: Mutation: negate the confirmed branch — confirmed tool calls would go to PendingConfirmation instead of Running.
assertion: SessionToolCallReady with confirmed=undefined produces status PendingConfirmation; with confirmed produces Running

concept: SessionToolCallReady is no-op unless status is Streaming or Running

citation.excerpt: if (tc.status !== ToolCallStatus.Streaming && tc.status !== ToolCallStatus.Running) { return tc; }

notes: Mutation: remove guard — a Cancelled tool call could transition to Ready state.
assertion: SessionToolCallReady on a Completed tool call leaves it Completed

concept: SessionToolCallConfirmed with approved=true transitions to Running
notes: Mutation: negate approved — approved would produce Cancelled. Operator: negate-conditional.
assertion: SessionToolCallConfirmed approved=true yields status Running with correct toolInput

concept: SessionToolCallConfirmed with approved=false transitions to Cancelled
notes: Mutation: set Completed instead of Cancelled. Operator: replace-value.
assertion: SessionToolCallConfirmed approved=false yields status Cancelled with reason and reasonMessage

concept: SessionToolCallConfirmed resolves selectedOption from tc.options by selectedOptionId
notes: Mutation: remove resolveSelectedOption call — selectedOption would be undefined in the output. Operator: remove-call.
assertion: After SessionToolCallConfirmed with selectedOptionId, the resulting state has selectedOption matching that id

concept: SessionToolCallConfirmed is no-op unless status is PendingConfirmation
notes: Mutation: remove the guard — Running tool calls could be double-confirmed.
assertion: SessionToolCallConfirmed on a Running tool call leaves it Running

concept: SessionToolCallComplete transitions Running tool call to Completed
notes: Mutation: always set Completed ignoring requiresResultConfirmation flag. Operator: remove-conditional.
assertion: SessionToolCallComplete with requiresResultConfirmation=true produces PendingResultConfirmation; false or absent produces Completed

concept: SessionToolCallComplete carries confirmed from Running state, NotNeeded from PendingConfirmation

citation.excerpt: const confirmed = tc.status === ToolCallStatus.Running ? tc.confirmed : ToolCallConfirmationReason.NotNeeded;

notes: Mutation: always use NotNeeded regardless of status. Or swap Running/PendingConfirmation branches.
assertion: completed.confirmed preserves the value from the Running phase; from PendingConfirmation it is NotNeeded

concept: SessionToolCallResultConfirmed with approved=true produces Completed
notes: Mutation: negate approved — approved=true would produce Cancelled.
assertion: SessionToolCallResultConfirmed approved=true yields status Completed

concept: SessionToolCallResultConfirmed with approved=false produces Cancelled with reason ResultDenied
notes: Mutation: use a different cancellation reason. Operator: replace-value.
assertion: SessionToolCallResultConfirmed approved=false yields status Cancelled with reason ResultDenied

concept: SessionToolCallContentChanged is no-op unless tool call is Running
notes: Mutation: remove the Running guard — completed tool calls could have their content overwritten.
assertion: SessionToolCallContentChanged on a Completed tool call leaves content unchanged

concept: SessionTitleChanged updates summary.title and modifiedAt

citation.excerpt: return { ...state, summary: { ...state.summary, title: action.title, modifiedAt: Date.now() }, };

notes: Mutation: remove modifiedAt update — title changes would not mark the session modified. Or assign empty string to title.
assertion: After SessionTitleChanged, summary.title === action.title AND summary.modifiedAt is updated

concept: SessionUsage is no-op when active turn id does not match
notes: Mutation: remove the turnId check — usage from a stale turn would overwrite the active turn's usage.
assertion: SessionUsage with wrong turnId leaves activeTurn.usage unchanged

concept: SessionReasoning appends content to existing Reasoning part

citation.excerpt: if (part.kind === ResponsePartKind.Reasoning) { return { ...part, content: part.content + action.content }; }

notes: Mutation: replace with assignment — reasoning content replaced not appended. Or remove the Reasoning kind guard.
assertion: After two SessionReasoning deltas, reasoning part content equals both concatenated

concept: SessionIsReadChanged sets or clears the IsRead bit via withStatusFlag
notes: Mutation: pass IsArchived instead of IsRead — wrong flag modified. Operator: replace-argument.
assertion: SessionIsReadChanged isRead=true sets IsRead bit; isRead=false clears it without touching other bits

concept: SessionIsArchivedChanged sets or clears the IsArchived bit via withStatusFlag
notes: Mutation: pass IsRead instead of IsArchived. Or negate action.isArchived. Operator: replace-argument or negate-argument.
assertion: SessionIsArchivedChanged isArchived=true sets IsArchived bit only

concept: SessionConfigChanged uses replace to overwrite or merges into existing config values
notes: Mutation: always replace (remove ternary). A merge scenario would drop existing keys. Operator: remove-ternary.
assertion: SessionConfigChanged with replace=false merges; replace=true overwrites all existing values

concept: SessionConfigChanged is no-op when state.config is absent
notes: Mutation: remove the guard — config would be set from undefined, creating a broken config object.
assertion: SessionConfigChanged when state.config is undefined returns state unchanged

concept: SessionTruncated with undefined turnId clears all turns
notes: Mutation: remove the undefined check — turns would always be slice-to-idx. Operator: remove-branch.
assertion: SessionTruncated with no turnId produces an empty turns array

concept: SessionTruncated with turnId slices turns up to and including that turn
notes: Mutation: use idx instead of idx+1 — the named turn would be excluded. Or return full array on idx<0.
assertion: SessionTruncated keeps the named turn and all prior turns, removing everything after

concept: SessionInputRequested appends a new request when id is not found

citation.excerpt: function upsertInputRequest(state: SessionState, request: SessionInputRequest): SessionState { const existing = state.inputRequests ?? []; const idx = existing.findIndex(r => r.id === request.id);

notes: Mutation: skip upsert — always append, creating duplicates. Operator: remove-conditional.
assertion: SessionInputRequested with duplicate id replaces not duplicates the entry

concept: SessionInputRequested update preserves existing answers when updating a request

citation.excerpt: if (idx >= 0) { const answers = request.answers ?? inputRequests[idx].answers; inputRequests[idx] = { ...request, answers }; }

notes: Mutation: always use request.answers (ignoring existing) — existing draft answers lost on server re-send.
assertion: SessionInputRequested on an existing request id preserves previously set answers when new request.answers is undefined

concept: SessionInputCompleted removes the matching request from inputRequests
notes: Mutation: negate the filter predicate — the matching request survives, all others are removed.
assertion: After SessionInputCompleted, the request with matching id is absent from inputRequests

concept: SessionInputCompleted is no-op when the request id is not present
notes: Mutation: remove the guard — filtering an absent id would still produce a new array object, creating spurious updates.
assertion: SessionInputCompleted with unknown requestId returns the same state reference

concept: SessionInputCompleted clears inputRequests entirely when last request is removed
notes: Mutation: always assign inputRequests even when empty — field would be [] instead of absent.
assertion: After removing the last input request, state.inputRequests is undefined not []

concept: SessionPendingMessageSet routes to steeringMessage or queuedMessages based on kind
notes: Mutation: remove the kind check — steering messages go into the queued list. Operator: remove-branch.
assertion: SessionPendingMessageSet with kind=steering sets steeringMessage; kind=queued appends to queuedMessages

concept: SessionPendingMessageRemoved for steering is no-op when id does not match steeringMessage

citation.excerpt: if (action.kind === PendingMessageKind.Steering) { if (!state.steeringMessage || state.steeringMessage.id !== action.id) { return state; }

notes: Mutation: remove the id check — any steering-kind removal would clear steeringMessage regardless of id.
assertion: SessionPendingMessageRemoved with steering kind and wrong id leaves steeringMessage unchanged

concept: SessionPendingMessageRemoved returns same reference when filtered list is unchanged

citation.excerpt: return filtered.length === existing.length ? state : { ...state, queuedMessages: filtered.length > 0 ? filtered : undefined };

notes: Mutation: always return new state object — spurious updates when id not found. Or fail to clear when list becomes empty.
assertion: SessionPendingMessageRemoved with unknown id returns same state reference (no spurious update)

concept: SessionQueuedMessagesReordered reorders list per action.order then appends unlisted messages
notes: Mutation: ignore dedup guard (ordered.has) — duplicate entries in action.order would produce duplicates in output.
assertion: SessionQueuedMessagesReordered with duplicate ids in action.order does not duplicate queue entries

concept: SessionQueuedMessagesReordered appends messages not in order list at the end in original order
notes: Mutation: remove the append loop — messages not in action.order would be lost.
assertion: SessionQueuedMessagesReordered preserves messages not mentioned in order, appended after the ordered ones

concept: summaryStatus derives InputNeeded when inputRequests or pending confirmation exist
notes: Mutation: remove the inputRequests check — pending requests without pending confirmation would show InProgress. Operator: remove-disjunct.
assertion: Session with one pending inputRequest and one active turn shows status InputNeeded not InProgress

concept: summaryStatus derives Idle when no activeTurn and no pending confirmation
notes: Mutation: remove Idle branch — no-turn sessions would get InProgress. Operator: remove-branch.
assertion: After SessionTurnComplete, status returns to Idle when no other activity remains

concept: summaryStatus preserves orthogonal bits (IsRead, IsArchived) when updating activity
notes: Mutation: replace with just activity — IsRead and IsArchived bits would be lost on any activity change. Operator: simplify-expression.
assertion: When activity changes (turn starts), IsRead and IsArchived flags are preserved in the new status value

concept: hasPendingToolCallConfirmation checks both PendingConfirmation and PendingResultConfirmation
notes: Mutation: remove PendingResultConfirmation check — sessions waiting for result confirmation would show InProgress not InputNeeded. Operator: remove-disjunct.
assertion: Session with a PendingResultConfirmation tool call shows status InputNeeded

concept: rootReducer RootConfigChanged respects replace flag same as session version
notes: Mutation: always replace — merge semantics lost for root config. Operator: remove-ternary.
assertion: RootConfigChanged with replace=false merges with existing root config values

concept: rootReducer RootConfigChanged is no-op when root state has no config
notes: Mutation: remove guard — config created from undefined. Operator: remove-conditional.
assertion: RootConfigChanged when state.config is absent returns state unchanged

concept: TerminalData appends to existing unclassified tail part

citation.excerpt: } else if (tail && tail.type === 'unclassified') { content[content.length - 1] = { ...tail, value: tail.value + action.data }; }

notes: Mutation: replace with assignment — unclassified data replaced not appended. Operator: replace-operator.
assertion: Two TerminalData actions with unclassified tail produce one part with both data values concatenated

concept: TerminalData appends to output of an in-progress command part

citation.excerpt: if (tail && tail.type === 'command' && !tail.isComplete) { content[content.length - 1] = { ...tail, output: tail.output + action.data };

notes: Mutation: remove isComplete check — completed commands would also have output appended. Or replace + with replacement.
assertion: TerminalData appends to command output only when command is not complete

concept: TerminalCommandFinished marks only the matching commandId as complete
notes: Mutation: remove commandId check — all commands finished. Or omit exitCode. Operator: remove-conditional, remove-field.
assertion: TerminalCommandFinished with commandId X marks only that command complete; others stay incomplete

concept: ChangesetFileSet upserts: appends new file or replaces in-place by id

citation.excerpt: const idx = state.files.findIndex(f => f.id === action.file.id); if (idx < 0) { return { ...state, files: [...state.files, action.file] }; }

notes: Mutation: always append — existing files would accumulate duplicates. Operator: remove-conditional.
assertion: ChangesetFileSet with existing file id replaces it at the same index; new id appends

concept: ChangesetFileRemoved is no-op when fileId is not found

citation.excerpt: const idx = state.files.findIndex(f => f.id === action.fileId); if (idx < 0) { return state; }

notes: Mutation: remove the guard — splice(idx, 1) where idx=-1 would remove the last element.
assertion: ChangesetFileRemoved with unknown fileId leaves files array unchanged

concept: ChangesetStatusChanged carries error only when new status is Error; otherwise strips it

citation.excerpt: if (action.status === ChangesetStatus.Error) { return { ...state, status: action.status, error: action.error }; } const { error: _ignored, ...rest } = state;

notes: Mutation: always keep error field — stale errors survive status recovery. Operator: remove-branch.
assertion: ChangesetStatusChanged to a non-Error status strips any previous error from the state

concept: ChangesetOperationStatusChanged strips error field from operation when status is not Error
notes: Mutation: always carry error — stale error persists on a recovered operation. Operator: remove-branch.
assertion: After ChangesetOperationStatusChanged to Running, operation.error is absent

concept: ChangesetCleared is a reference-preserving no-op when files is already empty
notes: Mutation: remove the guard — always allocate new state object, producing spurious updates.
assertion: ChangesetCleared on already-empty state returns the same state reference

concept: host.mjs initialize handler returns snapshot with current serverSeq and state

citation.excerpt: ws.send(JSON.stringify({ jsonrpc: '2.0', id: msg.id, result: { protocolVersion: '0.3.0', serverSeq: s.serverSeq, snapshots: [{ resource: channel, state: s.state, fromSeq: s.serverSeq }] } }))

notes: Mutation: omit serverSeq from result — client cannot detect gaps. Or use hardcoded 0.
assertion: initialize result includes serverSeq equal to current channel sequence, and snapshots array with the channel resource

concept: host.mjs streams all planned actions as notifications after sending initialize result

citation.excerpt: for (const a of plan) ws.send(JSON.stringify({ jsonrpc: '2.0', method: 'action', params: ch.apply(a) }))

notes: Mutation: omit action notifications — client state diverges from host. Or send in wrong method name.
assertion: After initialize, client receives N action notifications matching the plan length

concept: host.mjs subscribe handler returns snapshot with current state at current serverSeq

citation.excerpt: } else if (msg.method === 'subscribe') { const s = ch.snapshot() ws.send(JSON.stringify({ jsonrpc: '2.0', id: msg.id, result: { snapshot: { resource: msg.params?.channel ?? channel, state: s.state, fromSeq: s.serverSeq } } }))

notes: Mutation: use empty state instead of ch.snapshot().state — client diverges immediately. Or omit fromSeq.
assertion: subscribe result contains the channel's current state and the current fromSeq/serverSeq

concept: host.mjs increments serverSeq monotonically on each applied action

citation.excerpt: apply: (action) => { state = sessionReducer(state, action); return { channel, action, serverSeq: ++seq, origin: null } },

notes: Mutation: remove ++ (use seq without increment) — all notifications carry same serverSeq 0. Operator: remove-prefix-operator.
assertion: Each action notification has a strictly increasing serverSeq starting from 1

concept: host.mjs silently ignores unknown methods (no response sent)
notes: Mutation: add an else-send error response — host would actively reject unknown methods, changing protocol behavior for future methods.
assertion: A request with an unknown method produces no response from this minimal host (forward-compat behaviour)

concept: SessionCustomizationToggled is no-op when customization id is not found

citation.excerpt: const idx = list.findIndex(c => c.id === action.id); if (idx < 0) { return state; }

notes: Mutation: remove the guard — splice or update on idx=-1 would corrupt the list.
assertion: SessionCustomizationToggled with unknown id returns state unchanged

concept: SessionCustomizationRemoved searches nested children when top-level id not found
notes: Mutation: skip the nested search — removing a child customization would be a no-op if only checked at top level.
assertion: SessionCustomizationRemoved with a child customization id removes it from the container's children array
